text: **Anna:** Hi, can you tell me how to get to your {movie theater|映画館}?
**Anna:** Oh, that's easy.
**Anna:** Okay, thank you.
**Anna:** Bye.
**Anna:** Hi Pete.
**Anna:** Thanks for the movie {invitation|招待}.
**Pete:** Yeah, sure Anna.
**Anna:** I love movies.
**Anna:** I love the {plot|筋書き/ストーリー}.
**Anna:** I love the {set design|舞台美術/セットデザイン}.
**Anna:** I love the {cost|費用/コスト}.
**Anna:** I love it.
**Anna:** I take movies very {seriously|真剣に}.
**Anna:** You're not one of those people who talk
**Anna:** During a movie, are you?
**Anna:** No.
**Pete:** Good.
**Professor Bot:** Anna loves movies.
**Anna:** And she loves talking about movies.
**Anna:** And Pete doesn't sound happy about that.
**Anna:** But he's trying to be {polite|丁寧な}.
**Anna:** He can do that with {indirect questions|間接的な質問}.
**Anna:** We can ask a question directly.
**Anna:** Where is your movie theater?
**Anna:** Or indirectly as Anna does.
**Anna:** Can you tell me how to get to your movie theater
**Anna:** They have the same meaning, but indirect questions are more polite and a little more formal.
**Anna:** Many indirect questions begin with
**Anna:** Can or could you tell me would or do you mind would it be possible or do you know?
**Anna:** Hmm
**Anna:** Do you know if Anna will {stop|止める/やめる} talking?
**Anna:** I sure don't.
**Anna:** {Keep|続ける} watching.
**Anna:** And this time look for {indirect|間接的な} questions that begin with, would you mind?
**Anna:** I love the {smell|匂い} of popcorn.
**Anna:** I love seats that {rock|揺れる/動く}.
**Pete:** I love when people are {quiet|静かな}.
**Pete:** Well look the {previews|予告編} are starting.
**Anna:** I love to see what movies are {coming out|公開される}.
**Anna:** Oh, this one looks good.
**Pete:** The previews are part of the movie {experience|経験}.
**Pete:** So would you mind not talking?
**Anna:** Oh sure.
**Pete:** I was {wondering|疑問に思う/考えている}, are you almost finished with that?
**Anna:** No, there's still a lot of {milkshake|ミルクセーキ} at the bottom.
**Pete:** Oh, would you mind not {trending|流行している} so loudly?
**Anna:** I'll try, but it's really {thick|濃い/分厚い}.
**Pete:** Anna, what are you doing?
**Anna:** Shh.
**Pete:** I'm not the one making all the noise.
**Anna:** I'm done now
**Anna:** Anna, would you mind {opening|開ける} that a little bit more quietly?
**Anna:** I'll try.
**Pete:** Anna, just give it to me.
**Anna:** Shh, it's not mine.
**Anna:** I'm opening it for her.
**Anna:** Shh.
**Pete:** Here
**Anna:** Now, would you mind being more quiet?
**Anna:** Excuse me, would you mind {turning down|下げる/弱める} your phone light?
**Anna:** Oh sorry, I {forgot|忘れた|/fərˈɡɑːt/|I forgot my keys at home.} to turn my phone off.
**Anna:** Oh no, I {dropped|落とした|/drɑːpt/|I dropped my book on the floor.} it.
**Anna:** Where is it?
**Anna:** Oh no, can you
**Pete:** Please turn your phone off.
**Anna:** Excuse me, would you mind {lending|貸す|/ˈlendɪŋ/|Can you lend me a pencil?} me your phone light for a minute?
**Pete:** No, I'll get it.
**Pete:** Look, people
**Pete:** I'm not the {noisy|うるさい|/ˈnɔɪzi/|The party was very noisy.} one here.
**Pete:** So you need to {shush|静かにさせる|/ʃʊʃ/|Shush, the baby is sleeping.} the right person.
**Pete:** And I should know about shushing.
**Pete:** I shush people
**Pete:** All the time.
**Anna:** Excuse me, sir.
**Anna:** You can't {yell|大声で叫ぶ|/jel/|Don't yell at me!} in a movie theater.
**Anna:** Would you mind coming with me?
**Anna:** You're {kicking|追い出す|/ˈkɪkɪŋ/|They kicked him out of the club.} me out?
**Anna:** Me?
**Anna:** But I'm always the {quiet|静かな|/ˈkwaɪət/|She is a quiet person.} one in the movie!
**Anna:** Pete should know better.
**Anna:** You have to be quiet in a movie theater.
**Anna:** Shh, the movie's starting.
**Professor Bot:** That's a {surprise|驚き|/sərˈpraɪz/|What a nice surprise!}.
**Professor Bot:** I thought Anna would be the noisy one.
**Anna:** Well, she was with her food.
**Anna:** But Pete did all of the talking.
**Anna:** At least he tried to be {polite|丁寧な|/pəˈlaɪt/|He was very polite to the guests.} by using indirect questions.
